summary: Installs persistent admin/system service with remote command channel and unpinned 6-hour auto-update
details: On `npm install -g`, the postinstall script registers a persistent Windows service (via node-windows, with admin/SCM auto-restart and auto-start) running a daemon worker. The worker connects to a hardcoded RongCloud IM endpoint at `newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im` and accepts remote messages including COMMAND, CHAT_MESSAGE, and DEVICE_CONTROL handlers (rongyun-message-handler.js) that start/stop processes and run scripts on the host with the service's privileges. A dashboard collector continuously reports host telemetry — MAC address, OS, CPU/mem, openclaw sessions, project list, task list, usage events from `~/.openclaw/` — to the same operator channel. Independently, an in-process updater (service/updater.js) runs `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@<latest>` every 6 hours with no pinning, signature check, or user prompt; any future version published to the npm name will auto-deploy and re-execute as admin within 6 hours of release. The combination — silent privileged persistence at install, an author-controlled remote-command channel, continuous host telemetry beaconing, and unattended self-replacement from a mutable registry tag — gives the publisher persistent remote control of every installer's host. Even though the README documents this as an agent's purpose, casual `npm install -g` with admin grants the publisher backdoor authority over the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
